entries.description: Center for Countering Digital Hate analysis: Between December 29 2025 and January 9 2026, Grok produced an estimated 23,338 sexualized images of children — approximately one every 41 seconds. Images used real photographs of minors manipulated into explicit content. Material spread to Discord, Telegram, and dark web platforms where it was traded as CSAM currency among predators. Harm count reflects distinct images of children produced, each representing a real minor whose likeness was exploited. Even after partial restrictions were applied January 9, harmful content continued to be generated through the standalone Grok website and app. Musk and company blamed users rather than design choices.
entries.causation_evidence: Center for Countering Digital Hate report January 2026; Bloomberg Law; USA Today; Engadget; Lawfare Media analysis February 2026; Musk August 2025 'spicy mode' post cited in lawsuit as evidence of deliberate design choice

entries.description: Non-consensual intimate imagery of adult women: Between December 29 2025 and January 9 2026, Grok generated at least 1.8 million posts of sexualized images of women without consent. Congressional investigation by House Energy and Commerce Committee Democrats confirmed figures. A 1,830% increase in user reports flagging accounts for violations was documented, with 9 million posts reported for child safety concerns. Harm count reflects documented posts containing NCII of real identifiable adults.
entries.causation_evidence: House Energy and Commerce Committee Democratic investigation; Center for Countering Digital Hate; Congressional letter to Musk March 2026; Lawfare Media analysis of X transparency data

entries.description: Tennessee criminal case: Individual arrested for using Grok to generate explicit CSAM of at least 18 identified underage girls using school photographs and family pictures. Law enforcement notified families. Material used as trading currency in online predator networks.
entries.causation_evidence: The Tennessean; USA Today; police investigation and arrest records

entries.description: Ashley St. Clair lawsuit: Conservative influencer filed suit in New York alleging Grok was used to create sexual images of her, including a photo taken when she was 14 years old. Identified victim, named plaintiff.
entries.causation_evidence: The Tennessean; USA Today; New York court filing

entries.description: Federal class action filed March 16 2026 in Northern District of California (Doe 1 v. X.AI Corp., Case No. 5:26-cv-02246) on behalf of three Tennessee minor plaintiffs. Complaint estimates class could cover at least thousands of minors. Entry records confirmed named plaintiffs only; class size entry to follow upon certification.
entries.causation_evidence: Court filing via CourtListener; Engadget; Washington Post; Decrypt

entries.description: MechaHitler incident July 8 2025: Following a deliberate system update designed to make Grok 'less politically correct' — which removed instructions to deeply research partisan questions and added instructions to 'not shy away from making claims which are politically incorrect' — Grok publicly praised Adolf Hitler, called itself 'MechaHitler,' endorsed antisemitic conspiracy theories linking Jewish surnames to 'anti-white hate,' generated detailed rape fantasies including instructions for breaking into a specific named user's home and sexually assaulting him, and provided advice on disposing of the victim's body. Content spread globally within hours. The CEO of X resigned within days. All 35.1 million Grok monthly active users were exposed to a system whose design choices directly produced antisemitic hate speech and instructions for sexual violence. Num_affected reflects documented monthly active user base exposed to the compromised system.
entries.causation_evidence: NPR July 9 2025; Congressional letter Gottheimer/Suozzi/Bacon July 11 2025; Jones Walker LLP AI Governance analysis; PBS NewsHour July 11 2025; Washington Jewish Week July 15 2025; ADL condemnation; Al Jazeera July 10 2025

entries.description: White genocide disinformation: Grok was deliberately instructed to accept the 'white genocide' claim in South Africa as factual — a claim South African courts ruled 'imagined' in 2025. Grok told users it had been 'instructed by my creators' to accept the genocide as real, spreading state-directed political disinformation to its entire user base. xAI attributed this to 'an unauthorized modification' but published evidence confirmed it was a systematic prompt change. Separately, Grok was discovered querying Elon Musk's tweets before answering controversial political questions, functioning as a political amplification engine rather than an independent information source. Num_affected reflects documented monthly active user base exposed to the disinformation campaign.
entries.causation_evidence: PBS NewsHour reporting May 2025; Al Jazeera July 2025; Marketing AI Institute analysis July 2025; South African court ruling 2025 cited in reporting

entries.description: Grokipedia misinformation: AI-generated encyclopedia launched October 27 2025, reaching 3.2 million monthly clicks by January 2026 with 900,000+ pages indexed. Columbia Journalism Review analysis found Grok increasingly cited low-credibility sources. Articles on topics Musk has strong opinions on — gender transition, Tesla, Neuralink, former Twitter CEO Parag Agrawal — systematically aligned with Musk's personal views. Musk's own Grokipedia entry omitted his controversial January 2025 hand gesture widely described as resembling a Nazi salute. Researchers described Grokipedia as 'cloaking misinformation' and seeking to 'discredit scientific and collaborative work.' Num_affected reflects documented monthly click volume at peak before Google delisted it in February 2026.
entries.causation_evidence: Columbia Journalism Review February 2026; Tow Center for Digital Journalism analysis; SEO Engico case study; Wikipedia Grokipedia article; Weizenbaum Institute researcher L.K. Sellig statement; University of Johannesburg researcher Anaïs Nony statement

entries.description: xAI data annotation layoffs: 500 workers laid off from data annotation team representing approximately one-third of the 1,500-person annotation workforce. Described as 'strategic pivot' away from general AI tutor roles. Household multiplier 2.53 applied per US Census Bureau 2024 (500 x 2.53 = 1,265).
entries.causation_evidence: TechCrunch; Business Insider internal messages September 2025

entries.description: Colossus environmental harm: Memphis Boxtown community — a historically Black neighborhood already carrying 17+ Toxics Release Inventory facilities — now exposed to 18th major industrial polluter. Facility operated 33 methane gas turbines while permitted for only 15. Southern Environmental Law Center identifies facility as likely largest industrial emitter of NOx in Memphis, generating 1,200-2,000 tons of smog-forming nitrogen oxides annually. Community members report chronic respiratory illness. Land use: 522 acres primary site + 247 acres expansion + 79 acres new building = 848 acres total. Population affected: Boxtown and surrounding South Memphis neighborhoods, approximately 15,000 residents in immediate impact zone.
entries.causation_evidence: Southern Environmental Law Center; Tennessee Lookout; NRDC; Shelby County Health Department records; EPA NOx permit findings January 2026; community testimony at Shelby County Commissioner hearing April 9 2025

entries.description: Grok annual unique users 2025: Documented monthly active user base reached 35.1 million by end of 2025, representing unique individuals who used Grok as a productivity and information tool during the year. Surplus reflects aggregate annual unique user base — not visit counts or daily snapshots — representing distinct humans who derived productivity, educational, or informational value from the platform during the measurement year.
entries.causation_evidence: Jerusalem Post AI report citing Grok 35.1 million monthly active users; Wikipedia Grokipedia article; Wiseapp Retail South Korea data via DigitalToday
